rules.why: Session #053 (INC-BUDGETRATE-EQG): the prior 8-member framing was incomplete. Real composite has 15 members + 1 BAdI. Bug isn't 'gate rejects USD everywhere' - it's gate input source asymmetry. Only Camp B mis-handles cross-currency.
rules.how_to_apply: When triaging FMAVC005 or any BR-related question, list all 15 composite members + classify into camps before reasoning about behavior. Don't trust prior 8-member docs.

rules.rule: Before claiming any custom Z/Y object affects production behavior, query TADIR + REPOSRC against P01 (production) directly via RFC. D01-only or V01-only objects do NOT affect production no matter how interesting their source code looks. Cross-check via: SELECT FROM TADIR WHERE OBJ_NAME=<X> on every system in the landscape (D01/V01/TS1/TS3/P01).
rules.why: Session #053: I claimed BR_AVC_EXCLUSIONS BAdI was suppressing AVC for MIRO/F110 in production based on D01 source. User caught it: 'I do not see it in TS3'. Cross-check confirmed it lives only in D01+V01, was deactivated 1 day after creation, never transported. Wrong claim wasted user time.
rules.how_to_apply: For any custom object analysis: (1) confirm presence in P01 first, (2) check ENHLOG for deactivation, (3) check transports in E070/E07T for release status, (4) only THEN claim it affects production behavior.

rules.rule: KBLE / KBLEW (Earmarked Fund consumption history) are cluster tables - RFC_READ_TABLE returns TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA. Use RFC-enabled wrapper: conn.call('/SAPPSPRO/PD_GM_FMR2_READ_KBLE', I_BELNR=<FR_doc>) which returns T_KBLE (consumption events with RBELNR/RBUZEI = consumer FI doc/line) AND T_KBLEW (per-currency split with CURTP=00 transaction currency, CURTP=10 local USD).
rules.why: Session #053: needed FR<->FI consumption traceability without BSEG.KBLNR. Wrapper found via /SAPPSPRO/ namespace probe. Returns 263 rows for one FR including the RBELNR linkage we couldn't get any other way.
rules.how_to_apply: For any FR consumption analysis: use wrapper FM, not RFC_READ_TABLE. KBLEW.CURTP=10 gives local-currency (USD) view directly; CURTP=00 gives transaction currency.

rules.rule: FMAVCT (BCS AVC totals) is a wide table (>512 bytes per row). RFC_READ_TABLE with FIELDS=[] returns DATA_BUFFER_EXCEEDED. Workaround: pass narrow FIELDS list (max 10-15 columns); split WHERE conditions into multiple OPTIONS entries (single complex boolean WHERE rejected with OPTION_NOT_VALID). Dedup result rows by full-row signature (RVERS/RPMAX often produce duplicates that double-count if summed naively). For AVC available calc: ALLOCTYPE_9='KBFC' = carryforward allotment (UNESCO ledger 9H 2026). HSL01-HSL16 are per-period local-currency values; HSLVT is yearly total.
rules.why: Session #053: needed AVC budget per fund for 26 funds. First attempts failed with parser errors. Final pattern works reliably: split WHERE + narrow FIELDS + dedup.
rules.how_to_apply: For any FMAVCT query in a script: (1) use DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET first to confirm column names, (2) pick max 12 fields, (3) split WHERE into multiple OPTIONS lines, (4) dedup by row signature before summing. ALLOCTYPE_9 dimension distinguishes budget vs. consumption rows.

rules.rule: FMIOI WRTTP=81 rows for an Earmarked Fund include: original creation (positive), period-specific consumption reductions (negative), period-016 year-end reversal (positive equal to remaining), period-000 carryforward-IN to next year. Naive sign-sum across all years does NOT equal 'open balance'. For 'open' filter by current GJAHR + signed sum. For 'lifetime consumed' use KBLE/KBLEW (the consumption history), NOT FMIOI. FMIOI rows with ratio=1.0 and tiny amounts ($0.01-0.02) are SAP rounding plugs, NOT cross-currency consumption signatures.
rules.why: Session #053: misread the AFFECTED 20 lines as 'cross-currency consumption already fired' when they were actually rounding plugs. Also computed wrong open balance from FMIOI by naive subtraction. User caught both errors.
rules.how_to_apply: Always sample 3 raw FMIOI WRTTP=81 rows for any FR before computing per-line metrics. Identity-ratio rows with sub-cent amounts = rounding plugs, ignore. For consumption history, use KBLEW filtered by CURTP - not FMIOI signed sums.

rules.rule: When iterating on a wide table (markdown/excel/report), NEVER remove existing columns when adding new ones. Build the wider table by extending the previous column set in a single canonical script. If a column becomes irrelevant, mark it 'deprecated' but keep displaying it for one session before removing.
rules.why: Session #053: I removed columns 'actual EUR cons USD-eq', 'actual nonEUR USD cons', 'non-EUR breakdown' when adding 'AVC budget USD' + 'AVC available USD'. User caught it twice (the 2nd time with 'PUTA MADRE / me haces perder el tiempo / me reago' — strong frustration signal). This is a costly mistake that breaks user trust.
rules.how_to_apply: When updating a previously-shown table: (1) read the user's last shown column list, (2) the new table MUST contain >= those columns + the additions, (3) if you must restructure, show the column-mapping diff explicitly first and ask. Use a single source-of-truth script that builds incrementally so columns can never silently drop.

rules.rule: Every aggregation column in a report must explicitly state its filter scope in the header AND in any explanatory note. Allowed scopes: 'per-line' (REFBN+RFPOS), 'per-FONDS+FIPEX' (cover-group context), 'per-fund' (FONDS only), 'system-wide'. NEVER label an aggregation column 'total' without specifying 'total of WHAT'.
rules.why: Session #053: I labeled fund+FIPEX consumption as 'pool EUR cons' and the user reasonably assumed it was per-FR consumption ($170K vs €4,400 line). Caused 3 round-trips of clarification. The right column name was 'fund+FIPEX EUR cons (USD-equiv, broader cover-group context)'.
rules.how_to_apply: For any column derived from SUM/COUNT: name it with explicit scope marker. Examples: 'line cons EUR (this REFBN+RFPOS only)', 'pool USD cons (same FONDS+FIPEX, all FRs)'. Never use bare 'total' or 'cons' without scope qualifier.

rules.rule: In data-extraction scripts (RFC, SQL, file I/O) NEVER use 'except: pass' or 'except Exception: pass'. Silent failures cause downstream tables to show 0 / null without explanation, leading to wrong conclusions. ALWAYS at minimum: 'except Exception as e: print(f"ERROR <context>: {e}", file=sys.stderr)'. For RFC calls in particular, log the WHERE clause and parameters that failed.
rules.why: Session #053: my background FMAVCT script used 'except: pass' to swallow per-fund RFC errors. Result: AVC budget column showed 0 for ALL 64 rows (the WHERE clause syntax differed from earlier successful runs). User caught it ('AVC budget 0 doesn't make sense'). I would have spotted it instantly if errors had been printed.
rules.how_to_apply: grep -n 'except.*pass' on every extraction script before running. Replace with explicit error logging. For batch operations across N entities, log ERR + entity ID so you know which entity failed without re-running.

rules.rule: Before computing any metric over a SAP table (FMIOI, FMIFIIT, FMAVCT, BSEG, etc.), sample 3-5 RAW rows for one specific key and inspect them. This catches: (a) sign convention surprises (FMIOI carryforward pairs), (b) duplicate-row patterns (FMAVCT RVERS), (c) rounding-plug rows ($0.01-0.02 ratio=1.0 in FMIOI), (d) trailing-minus number formats ('1234.56-'). Skipping this step caused 3 wrong interpretations in Session #053.
rules.why: Session #053 mistakes: (1) interpreted $0.01 ratio=1.0 rows as cross-currency consumption (they were rounding plugs), (2) computed open balance as orig-consumed (sign convention wrong for carryforwards), (3) double-counted FMAVCT due to RVERS duplicates. All three would have been caught by 5 minutes of raw-row inspection upfront.
rules.how_to_apply: First action when querying a new table or new key: SELECT * FROM <table> WHERE <key>=<sample> ORDER BY ... LIMIT 10. Print rows. Read them. THEN write the aggregation. Total cost: 30 seconds. Total savings: hours of wrong analysis.

rules.rule: When producing a BCM signatory change spec for DBS, ALWAYS include ALL THREE identifiers: Rule number (90000004/90000005), RY OBJID (8-digit), and STEXT (exact group name). Never describe the target group by entity name alone.
rules.why: During INC-000006313 Part 2 cleanup (Session #052, 2026-04-13), DBS almost added Svein OESTTVEIT to IIEP Validation (RY 50010087) instead of UIS Validation (RY 50036801) — the two groups sit next to each other under rule 90000005, both end with the word 'Validation', and differ only by the prefix. Without the RY OBJID in the spec, DBS picked IIEP by mistake. Had the mis-add gone undetected, Svein would have been authorized to approve IIEP payments against the wrong bank carton (compliance breach). The mis-add was caught because the reconciliation check includes an off-entity ghost scan via the carton file.
rules.how_to_apply: Every change spec row must be a 5-tuple: (Action, Rule, RY_OBJID, STEXT, PERNR). Example: 'ADD, 90000005, 50036801, UIS Validation, 10097358'. When writing the spec, present RY OBJID prominently (not only in a footnote). Warn DBS explicitly about lookalike groups that sit next to the target in the OOCU_RESP tree — name at least one specific adjacent trap (e.g. 'do NOT pick IIEP Validation'). After DBS confirms execution, always re-run bcm_signatory_reconciliation_check.py — if the target RY has the expected members AND the adjacent-entity RYs (same rule, different prefix) are unchanged compared to the previous snapshot, the change was applied to the correct group.

rules.rule: At session close, every 'commitment', 'open follow-up', 'what's next', or 'consider building X' mentioned in the retro MUST have a corresponding PMO H-item before the session is closed. If a retro mentions N follow-up items, PMO must show N new/updated entries traceable to that retro.
rules.why: Session #053 generated 16 follow-up items in retro sections §7/§11/§13 (Brain updates, Knowledge graph edges, skill sap_fm_avc_intelligence, ZFIX object promotions, table registrations). Only 3 were formalized (H38/H39/H40). The other 13 quietly disappeared — discovered only at session #054 audit by the user. This is a systemic trap: retros are written with flow-of-thought commitment language but PMO is the enforcement layer. Without a bridge check, velocity-mode sessions erode the knowledge base silently.
rules.how_to_apply: Before marking a session closed, grep the retro for verbs of commitment (should, need to, must, will, consider, propose, promote, register) and for sections titled 'Open follow-ups', 'Brain updates needed', 'PMO updates', 'What's next'. For each unique action noun phrase, verify a matching H-item exists in PMO_BRAIN.md with session tag. If missing, either (a) add the H-item now, or (b) explicitly write 'DECIDED NOT TO TRACK: <reason>' in the retro so the absence is evidence of decision, not omission. When in doubt, formalize — a false-positive H-item can be killed; a false-negative follow-up is irreversible memory loss.

rules.rule: When extending a table, DataFrame, SQLite schema, Excel sheet, or any tabular artifact, NEVER drop existing columns while adding new ones. Use a single canonical script that all queries extend; columns are append-only unless the user explicitly asks to remove one.
rules.why: Session #053 retro §10 identified this as a 'did worse' pattern, observed twice in the same session: while building br_lines_FINAL.xlsx (INC-BUDGETRATE-EQG inventory), the agent added new analytical columns and silently dropped 3-4 existing ones the user had asked for. The user had to re-request and re-validate, which broke trust in the iteration. Root cause: agent was regenerating the whole DataFrame each iteration from a narrower column list instead of augmenting the existing one. Cost: loss of fidelity + wasted user time.
rules.how_to_apply: Maintain a canonical column list variable at the top of the extraction/build script. When adding a new column: append to the list, never replace it. When tempted to simplify: don't — preserve every column the user has ever asked for. If a column is truly obsolete, ask the user explicitly before dropping. In Excel output: pd.concat([existing_df, new_cols], axis=1), not pd.DataFrame(new_data_only). In SQLite: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, never DROP TABLE + CREATE TABLE. Verify column count is >= previous before saving.

rules.rule: Before computing aggregates (sum, avg, count, groupby) on an unfamiliar table or data shape, ALWAYS read 3+ raw rows first to verify sign convention, units, scale, and duplicate semantics. Write the aggregation ONLY after the raw sample is understood.
rules.why: Session #053 retro §10: the agent misinterpreted FMIOI sign convention twice — took absolute sums assuming all rows were positive, when in reality FMIOI WRTTP=81 rows for an FR include creation (positive), period-016 year-end reversal (negative), and period-000 carryforward-in (positive). Naive sum returned inflated commitment numbers. Same pattern with FMAVCT duplicates by RVERS/RPMAX. Root cause: the agent wrote the aggregation SQL before reading any raw row. For unfamiliar SAP tables with cluster or time-dependent semantics, this is guaranteed to produce wrong numbers.
rules.how_to_apply: For any new SAP table the agent queries for the first time in a session: (1) SELECT * FROM table LIMIT 5 with all fields visible; (2) inspect sign patterns of amount fields (HSL*, WRBTR, FKBTR) for the same key across rows; (3) check for version/status columns (RVERS, RPMAX, WRTTP, STATUS); (4) document sign convention in a comment before the aggregation SQL. For FMIOI specifically: always filter by GJAHR + WRTTP and trace by BUDAT for carryforward interpretation. For FMAVCT: dedup by RVERS/RPMAX before summing. For cluster tables (BSEG, KBLE, KBLEW): use the appropriate wrapper FM, never raw RFC_READ_TABLE.

rules.rule: Every aggregation column in a report, Excel sheet, or companion MUST explicitly state its filter in the column name or header comment: per-line, per-FR, per-fund, per-FONDS+FIPEX, per-year, per-company-code, per-ledger. Ambiguous 'total' or 'sum' is forbidden.
rules.why: Session #053 retro §10: during the INC-BUDGETRATE-EQG investigation, the agent presented a $170K number as if it were per-FR-line consumption when it was actually per-cover-group pool consumption (FONDS+FIPEX). The user asked 'but that doesn't match the Vonthron case which is one line' — this forced a correction and a re-explanation. The ambiguity was in a column named Total_Consumed which could have meant 4 different things. Unambiguous names at report time prevent misinterpretation at review time.
rules.how_to_apply: Column naming template: <metric>_<filter>_<unit>. Examples: committed_per_FR_USD, available_per_FONDS_FIPEX_USD, actual_per_line_EUR_2026_9H. In Excel headers: freeze row 1 + add a comment explaining the filter. In markdown tables: include filter in the column header, or add a one-line caption below. When in doubt, make the name longer, not shorter. At review: if the user asks 'but is this per X or per Y', the name failed — rename before answering.

evidence_of_violation.incident: $170K pool vs per-line consumption ambiguity, required correction

rules.rule: NEVER execute writes (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/BAPI commits/RFM calls with side effects) against P01 or any production system unless the user EXPLICITLY and unambiguously asks for that specific change on that specific day. 'Unblocked', 'executable', 'ready', 'recommended fix' describe STATE — they are NOT authorization to act. Default mode is evaluation / read-only.
rules.why: Session #055 (2026-04-14): when investigating H48, agent listed H45 as 'unblocked, executable'. User reacted with strong pushback: 'NO actualices NADA en PRD!!! SOlo estas evaluando!!!'. The framing of a task as 'executable' was being read as intent-to-execute. Production writes are high-blast-radius and irreversible — misreading state-language as authorization is a class of defect that must be prevented structurally.
rules.how_to_apply: 1) Any language about production fixes (H45/H46/H48 etc.) is EVALUATION only. 2) Before any RFC call that is not RFC_READ_TABLE, ask 'is this a write?'. If yes, stop and confirm in an explicit question to the user. 3) State writes in the FUTURE CONDITIONAL: 'si ejecutas', 'cuando decidas', never 'ejecuto', 'actualizo'. 4) Presentation of a fix (code diff, SQL INSERT, BAPI call) = design deliverable, never auto-implementation. 5) When user says 'ok proceder', verify they mean execute-write vs continue-investigation — if ambiguous, ask. 6) Applies to: P01 always. D01 if change is destructive or affects shared dev state. Quality_gate: any PR or script that writes to P01 must be approved line-by-line in chat before running.

rules.rule: In any custom ABAP program, `CALL TRANSACTION ... USING <bdc> MODE 'E'` (display on error) opens SAPGUI on every BDC error. When the CALL TRANSACTION sits inside a loop driven by `GET <table>` / `LDB_PROCESS` / large `LOOP AT`, the cumulative per-error GUI handshake consumes work-process time proportional to WAN RTT. On slow field-office paths this breaches `rdisp/max_wprun_time` and the dispatcher aborts the caller's LDB fetch with a TIME_OUT whose stack frame points at the LDB's own SELECT, not at the CALL TRANSACTION. Diagnose by checking (a) `CONSTANTS ... VALUE 'E'` declarations, (b) whether the BDC call site is inside a loop body, (c) whether the dump stack bottoms out in standard SAP LDB code (SAPDBSDF, SAPDBADA, etc.). Fix: MODE 'N' (silent) + `MESSAGES INTO <tab>` + post-loop error reporting in the list output. Canonical instance: YTBAE002.abap:27 + :723/:771/:819/:853 (INC-000006906, Claim 54).
rules.why: Session #058 (2026-04-20): INC-000006906 TIME_OUT dump at SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS was misdiagnosed for multiple sessions as (a) YBANK set coverage gap (Claim 52, falsified by source grep), (b) empty-range unbounded LDB scan (Claim 53, falsified by user's non-empty GR_SAKNR_OI), (c) data volume (13 BSIS rows, falsified). The dump hint text 'result set too large' and the LDB-code stack frame misdirected every early hypothesis. The real mechanism was only visible after user reproduced same program, same data, same user from HQ LAN in ~2 seconds vs Maputo WAN TIME_OUT. Three-path field reproduction (VPN / Paris / Maputo) isolated network as the decisive axis. Had this rule existed in the brain at the start of the investigation, the YTBAE002.abap:27 MODE 'E' declaration would have been the first code anchor checked, not the last.
rules.how_to_apply: 1) When triaging any TIME_OUT dump in an LDB callback (PUT_BSIS, PUT_BSAS, PUT_BSAD, PUT_BSIK, PUT_BSAK, PUT_BKPF, PUT_COEP, etc.), search the CALLER source for `MODE 'E'` and `CALL TRANSACTION` in parallel. 2) Flag any custom program with `CONSTANTS: <name> TYPE C VALUE 'E'` + at least one `CALL TRANSACTION ... MODE <that_const>` as NETWORK_COUPLED regardless of whether it has dumped yet. 3) Before recommending any other root cause for a field-office TIME_OUT, ask 'does the program open a GUI dialog on BDC error?'. 4) Fix pattern is always the same: MODE 'N' + `MESSAGES INTO tab` + `PERFORM add_to_accumulator USING tab` at each call site + render accumulator post-loop in the list output. YTBAE002's `PROC_RECONCIL_MESS_ADD` + `GT_RECONCIL_MESS` is the reference implementation. 5) Test every new recon / BDC-chained program from a field-office VPN before release; HQ LAN testing is necessary but not sufficient.

rules.rule: When D01 ADT HTTP Basic authentication returns 401 (password locked, expired, or account disabled), do NOT retry the ADT endpoint. Fall back to P01 SNC/SSO + pyrfc + `RPY_PROGRAM_READ` / `RPY_CLASS_READ` / `RPY_FUNCTIONMODULE_READ`. The same source code is visible in both systems because D01 and P01 remain in sync via transport; reading from P01 yields identical bytes for any object that has been promoted. The auth channel differs (SNC/Kerberos vs HTTP Basic), not the content.
rules.why: Session #057 (2026-04-20): JP_LOPEZ's D01 HTTP Basic password was locked during the INC-000006906 source-extraction step. Two options existed: (a) unlock the password and retry ADT, (b) switch to P01 SNC/SSO which was already working. Option (b) took ~3 minutes (import pyrfc, open connection, call RPY_PROGRAM_READ) and produced the same 3,422-line YTBAE002.abap source. Option (a) would have required a password-reset round-trip (~day). The pattern is re-usable: any time D01 ADT 401s, P01 RFC is a same-day alternative for any object that P01 has (which is true for anything released through the D01→TS1→TS3→P01 chain).
rules.how_to_apply: 1) On any D01 ADT HTTP 401 or 'password locked' response, do not retry ADT; switch to pyrfc + P01 SNC/SSO + RPY_PROGRAM_READ and continue. 2) The connection pattern is already documented in `Zagentexecution/sap_data_extraction/rfc_helpers.py`. 3) Write the extracted source to `extracted_code/...` normally; add a manifest note that source_system='P01' so the next agent knows. 4) Only retry D01 ADT if the object in question exists ONLY on D01 (e.g., in-flight customizing not yet transported). 5) Do not spend cycles troubleshooting D01 auth when a same-content alternative exists on P01.

rules.rule: TIME_OUT dumps in SAP-standard LDB callback forms (PUT_BSIS / PUT_BSAS / PUT_BSAD / PUT_BSIK / PUT_BSAK / PUT_BKPF / PUT_COEP / similar) frequently misdirect the analyst. The work-process IS waiting in that SELECT at the moment the dispatcher kills it, but the clock may have been consumed elsewhere: the CALLER's GET-handler doing a CALL TRANSACTION (MODE 'E' handshake over WAN — see `feedback_mode_e_bdc_is_network_coupling_risk`), a COMMIT WORK AND WAIT blocking on replication, an RFC roundtrip to a slow destination, or a synchronous HTTP call from a BAdI. Before filing an 'LDB performance tuning' ticket, inspect what the caller's handler (the GET event, the LDB_PROCESS callback, the NODES include) is doing for each row delivered.
rules.why: Session #058 (2026-04-20): INC-000006906 dump stack bottomed out at SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS. For multiple sessions this was read as 'LDB is slow on BSIS / YBANK set is miscoded / empty-range triggers full scan'. All three were wrong. The LDB was entirely fine; the caller (YTBAE002's GET bsis handler) was synchronously opening SAPGUI dialogs on BDC errors via MODE 'E' CALL TRANSACTIONs. The stack frame was an honest pointer to 'where the WP was waiting' but NOT to 'what consumed the time'.
rules.how_to_apply: 1) On any TIME_OUT whose deepest non-standard frame is still SAP-owned (include name starts with SAPDB, SAPL, SAPM, or RFBIBL / RSDB / etc.), pivot IMMEDIATELY to reading the CALLER's code: what program is driving the LDB? What does its GET-handler / callback form do for each row? 2) Specifically check for: `CALL TRANSACTION` (MODE 'E' is worst), `COMMIT WORK AND WAIT`, `CALL FUNCTION ... DESTINATION <remote>`, `CALL TRANSFORMATION`, synchronous BAdI dispatches with `cl_bad_*`. Each of these is a per-row cost multiplier. 3) Compute an order-of-magnitude estimate: if 1 row delivery triggers a 500 ms network round-trip and the LDB delivers 1,000 rows, the WP will exceed a 5-minute cap cleanly. 4) Only after the caller's handler is cleared should LDB tuning (indexes, WHERE pushdown, SELECT * vs INTO TABLE) be considered.

rules.rule: Every substantive agent<->user turn must be captured as a first-class interaction record in brain_v2/interactions/interactions.json with full preserved_full_text. Session-level retros are derived from this layer; they do NOT replace it.
rules.why: Session #055 almost lost the VRSD->E071 pivot knowledge; it survived only because the user asked. Session #058 captured INC-000006906 root cause via turn-level reasoning but only because the agent manually wrote each hypothesis. Violation mode: when a session ends abruptly, tentative hypotheses/corrections/user pushback evaporates — irreversible loss per CP-001. User explicitly directed Session #059: 'we can not compress or lose knowledge we need to efficiently relate every interaction and create more knowledge'.
rules.how_to_apply: Turn types to capture: hypothesis_test, correction, claim_draft, question_asked, question_answered, tool_trace, decision, user_pushback, user_knowledge_contribution, knowledge_preservation_directive. For each: assign INT-<sess>-<turn> ID, 3-axis domain, preserved_full_text (literal quote, NO summary), evidence list (typed refs), links to claims/rules/KUs/incidents/objects. Append to interactions.json immediately — do not batch at session close. Session close flushes _per_session_index, _per_domain_index, _per_object_index reverse indexes.

rules.rule: Every brain node (object, rule, claim, incident, known_unknown, data_quality, superseded, blind_spot, interaction, skill) must carry a non-null domain field using the 3-axis taxonomy {functional, module, process}. At least one axis must be populated. scope='global' is allowed only for truly cross-cutting rules (e.g., core_principles).
rules.why: User directive Session #059: 'Every topic can be assigned to a domain and improve the knowledge or use the knowledge'. Today only objects are domain-tagged (348 occurrences). Rules/claims/KUs/DQs/superseded/skills are untagged — making domain-scoped queries impossible. This is the reason knowledge routing feels ad-hoc: we know WHAT we learned but not WHERE it belongs.
rules.how_to_apply: 3 axes: functional (Treasury/FI/HCM/BCM/Payment/...), module (FI/HCM/TV/MM/BASIS/CTS/...), process (B2R/H2R/P2P/T2R/P2D). Backfill existing 90 rules, 54 claims, 7 incidents, 44 skills. On new node creation, refuse to persist without domain. brain_v2/domains/domains.json is the registry and carries reverse indexes per domain.

rules.rule: At session start, after reading brain_state.json, compute domain activation from the user's first substantive prompt using domains.json session_activation_hints. Auto-load knowledge_docs + skills + open KUs/DQs + companions for the activated domain(s). Log the activation decision as an interaction (type=decision).
rules.why: Without this, the agent rediscovers which skills/docs/KUs are relevant on every session — wastes cache tokens, misses links, and causes the 'context loaded but not used' failure mode (the problem Layer 11 incidents solved at its own granularity, now solved at domain granularity). User directive Session #059 anchors domains as the routing substrate.
rules.how_to_apply: 1. Scan user first prompt for keywords in domains.json._session_activation_hints regex map. 2. Produce ordered list of activated domains. 3. For each: read its knowledge_docs, note its skills, enumerate open KUs/DQs, reference its companions. 4. Announce 'Active domain(s)' in text before first tool call. 5. Persist the activation as INT-<sess>-000 interaction. 6. If no match: log as blind_spot candidate 'unclassified_session_scope'.

rules.rule: When migrating an XML payment format to ISO 20022 structured address, default to Hybrid (TwnNm+Ctry mandatory + AdrLine fallback) unless each specific bank spec demands fully-structured. Never assume all banks have identical strictness.
rules.why: CBPR+ Nov 2026 mandate permits Hybrid (structured + AdrLine coexist) as a valid compliance state. The handoff doc for UNESCO session #62 explicitly confirms TwnNm+Ctry are the only hard-required fields. Defaulting to Hybrid preserves rollback safety and accommodates vendor master DQ gaps (UNESCO 5% missing STREET, 68% missing HOUSE_NUM1 but 99.995% have CITY1+COUNTRY). Going to fully-structured by default wastes effort on vendor master cleanup that may not be required by all banks.
rules.how_to_apply: Before committing to 'only structured' scope, (1) collect each bank's written spec for structured-address strictness, (2) measure vendor master DQ per field to know the cost of fully-structured, (3) default to Hybrid per bank unless bank spec is explicit. Re-evaluate stricter modes post-go-live on a per-bank basis.

rules.rule: Structured-address migration in SAP DMEE has THREE distinct technical patterns by XML party, not one. Do not confuse them: (1) Cdtr address is SAP-auto-populated via FPAYHX Z-fields bound to vendor master LFA1+ADRC — DMEE tree config only, no ABAP needed. (2) Dbtr address is NOT in FPAYHX/FPAYH — requires ABAP user exit registered in OBPM4 Event 05 that reads T001→ADRC and writes FPAYHX-ZREF01..ZREF05 (NOT FPAYH-REF). (3) UltmtCdtr (alternative payee) may require a data source decision (vendor master / Z-table / separate user exit) per bank/format.
rules.why: UNESCO handoff doc §5 conflated the three patterns causing scope confusion in session #62. Dbtr requires a new FM while Cdtr does not. Mistaking the two leads to either unnecessary ABAP or missing ABAP — both are schedule-breakers for a Nov 2026 deadline.
rules.how_to_apply: When scoping a structured-address change, decompose per XML party (Dbtr/Cdtr/UltmtCdtr/CdtrAgt) and apply the correct pattern to each. Cdtr = tree-only. Dbtr = tree + user exit + OBPM4 registration. UltmtCdtr = decision-first, spec-second, code-third.

rules.rule: A user exit FM for DMEE can be registered in multiple PMW formats via OBPM4 simultaneously. Write once, reuse across formats. Avoid per-format FM duplication.
rules.why: Handoff doc recommends this; UNESCO has 3 target DMEE trees (SEPA_CT_UNES, CITI DC_V3_01, CGI_XML_CT_UNESCO) that all need the same Dbtr address enrichment logic. Building 3 FMs triples maintenance and drift risk.
rules.how_to_apply: When creating Z_DMEE_UNESCO_DEBTOR_ADDR or similar FMs, register it in OBPM4 for all needed PMW formats using Event 05. Centralize logic; format-specific tweaks go behind IF statements keyed on format name, not in separate FMs.

rules.rule: When designing a new ISO 20022 / pain.001 DMEE tree (or version) that needs structured address, source the structured nodes from FPAYHX-REF01/REF02/REF06 using SAP-std byte offsets — do NOT design a custom UNESCO BAdI for address. The SAP-std GENERIC class (CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_GENERIC) populates these REF buffers from ADRC at every Event 05 call: REF01[0..60]=street, [60..80]=building, [80..90]=post_code1, [90..100]=region, [100..110]=house_num1; REF02 has Cdtr equivalents; REF06[0..40]=city. Country classes (FR/DE/IT/GB) only ADD REF14 country-specific data, never override the address layout.
rules.why: Phase 0/1 expert re-evaluation discovered that SEPA structured address was thought to require new ABAP, but the SAP-std generic class already does the address-to-REF mapping universally. Avoiding custom address BAdI keeps the change surgical (config-only for SEPA) and aligned with SAP-supported pattern.
rules.how_to_apply: (1) For a new pain.001 tree: register a payment-medium FM (typically FI_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_CGI_05) for Event 05 in TFPM042FB. (2) Add DMEE nodes for StrtNm/BldgNb/PstCd/TwnNm/Ctry sourced from FPAYHX-REF01 with the byte-offset layout above (use MP_SC_FLD=REF01 + MP_OFFSET + appropriate length). (3) Verify in DMEE Tx Test mode with a sample FPAYHX payload before deploying to D01.

claims.claim: F-53 / FBZ2 manual posting fires the XREF substitution on EVERY BSEG line (vendor + bank GL + expense), but F110 automatic payment run fires it only on the vendor line (leaving bank GL XREF blank). claims.claim: UNESCO BR cross-currency drift bug = gate-input asymmetry across the 15-member ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE composite. Camp A (CHECK_CONS / EF_FUND_RESERVATION / FUNDBLOCK) gates on FR's own EUR currency and always fires. Camp B (PAYCOMMIT / NEW_ITEM / AVC / KBLEW / FI) gates on per-row posting currency and silently skips when posting is non-EUR. The two camps thus produce divergent ledger states: FR side at BR (1.09529), consumption side at identity / M-rate. AVC pool drifts fund-by-fund until cover-group hits zero headroom => FMAVC005.

evidence_for.cite: Source code: extracted_code/ENHO/ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE/ (15 enhancement files inspected line-by-line in Session #053). Production data: 64 BR-applicable open FR lines in P01 ledger 9H 2026, 35 with non-EUR consumption pool drift documented in Zagentexecution/incidents/INC_budget_rate_eq_guinea/br_lines_FINAL.xlsx.

claims.evidence_legacy_text_for: Source code: extracted_code/ENHO/ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE/ (15 enhancement files inspected line-by-line in Session #053). claims.claim: AVC available is a fund/cover-group level metric, not an FR-line-level metric. All FR lines on the same (FONDS, FUNDSCTR, CMMTITEM-rolled-to-control-object) draw from a single shared AVC pool. The pool is calculated as: Available = FMAVCT.allotment - sum(open FMIOI commitments) - sum(FMIFIIT actuals) on the same key. FMAVC005 fires when Available <= 0 + new posting amount > 0. The triggering FR line is incidental; the constraint is the fund pool.

evidence_for.cite: FMAVCT structure inspection + Vonthron's case empirical confirmation: fund 3110111021/PAX/TC 2026 has $12,897.80 carryforward allotment exactly matching the 5 carryforward FRs sum => Available = 0 => any USD posting on any of the 5 lines triggers FMAVC005.

claims.evidence_legacy_text_for: FMAVCT structure inspection + Vonthron's case empirical confirmation: fund 3110111021/PAX/TC 2026 has $12,897.80 carryforward allotment exactly matching the 5 carryforward FRs sum => Available = 0 => any USD posting on any of the 5 lines triggers FMAVC005.

claims.claim: YRGGBS00 UXR1 (XREF1 substitution, step 005) has DOUBLE ASYMMETRY vs UXR2 (XREF2, step 006): (1) CODE-LEVEL: line 998 has `IF bseg_xref1 = space` guard commented out, while UXR2 line 1026 keeps its guard intact; (2) CONFIG-LEVEL: UXR1 has NO declarative prerequisite in GB901 — fires unconditionally for every BSEG line, while UXR2 has prereq `HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021')` restricting to 6 specific GL accounts. Combined effect: UXR1 fires on EVERY posting line of EVERY user at UNESCO and unconditionally overwrites user-typed XREF1 with USR05.Y_USERFO. This is the root cause of the systemic XREF drift pattern observed in DQ-019 (21,754 manual Q1 2026 XREF edits via FBL3N/FB02/FBL5N). The manual edits are predominantly on XREF1 (universal), not XREF2 (targeted 6 accounts).

evidence_for.cite: `*  if bseg_xref1 = space.` commented out — UXR1 falls through to unconditional USR05 select + overwrite at line 1004
`IF bseg_xref2 = space.` active in UXR2 — guard maintained
RFC_READ_TABLE returns 0 rows — step 005 fires unconditionally (verified session #055)
RFC_READ_TABLE returns 3 rows: HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021') — step 006 only fires for 6 bank-related GLs (verified session #055)
16 substitution steps mapped to CONDID — confirms declarative architecture. Step 005=3UNESCO#005 (XREF1 prereq), Step 006=3UNESCO#006 (XREF2 prereq)
21,754 manual XREF post-posting edits in Q1 2026 on UNES BELEG documents via FBL3N/FBL1N/FB02/FBL5N — consistent with users correcting silently-overwritten XREF1 on every posting

claims.claim: YRGGBS00 UXR2 (XREF2 substitution, step 006) has a GL-account-restricted prerequisite: fires ONLY when BSEG.HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021') — 6 specific GL accounts stored in GB901 BOOLID '3UNESCO#006'. These 6 accounts are likely foreign-currency or bank-clearing accounts (GL pattern 137xxxx = intra-group, 1341xxx = bank-clearing). This selective firing explains why XREF2 auto-write only affects specific posting contexts, while XREF1 (step 005) fires universally (no prerequisite).

evidence_for.cite: SEQNR 01-03: HKONT = '1375012' OR HKONT = '1375022' OR HKONT = '1375032' OR HKONT = '1375052' OR HKONT = '1341011' OR HKONT = '1341021' (verified session #055 via RFC_READ_TABLE)

Extractions should list explicit SETLEAF fields (MANDT, SETCLASS, SUBCLASS, SETNAME, LINEID, VALSIGN, VALOPTION, VALFROM, VALTO, SEQNR); SETHEADER is 23 columns wide — pull with FIELDS=[].

claims.claim: YTBAE002 has an empty-RANGE-to-unbounded-LDB-scan defect at `YTBAE002.abap:1366-1370`. The LOOP AT LR_SAKNR that APPENDs each local SAKNR range entry to the COSEL/RSPARAMS table has NO `IS NOT INITIAL` guard. When the upstream SKB1 SELECT at `YTBAE002.abap:1098-1101` (WHERE BUKRS+HBKID+HKTID) returns zero rows that both (a) have SAKNR+3(2) IN ('11','13','15') AND (b) have XOPVW='X' (see IF chain at lines 1112-1118), the GR_SAKNR_OI range is empty, the loop iterates zero times, zero `SD_SAKNR` entries are pushed to LT_PARAMS, and `CALL FUNCTION 'LDB_PROCESS' LDBNAME='SDF'` at `YTBAE002.abap:1509-1517` runs BSIS with only `SD_BUKRS + SD_GJAHR + SD_STIDA + SD_CURTP` as filters. In SAP behavior an empty IN-list means no filter, so the LDB scans BSIS unrestricted by HKONT. At UNESCO that is 228,242 BSIS rows for BUKRS='UNES' GJAHR='2026' (live P01 RFC probe 2026-04-20), driving SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS past the dialog `rdisp/max_wprun_time` cap and terminating with runtime error TIME_OUT. Same program runs fine in batch (5 successful TBTCO runs March 2026, Status=F) because batch has no work-process time cap. This is the PRIMARY root-cause mechanism behind J_DAVANE's 2026-04-20 13:51:49 dump under TCODE YTR3.

evidence_for.cite: Session #057 RFC+SNC extraction — 3,422 lines. Scope SELECT at :1098-1101; OI routing at :1112-1118; missing-guard LOOP at :1366-1370; LDB call at :1509-1517. Zero INCLUDEs, zero Y/Z FM/class dependencies (only SAP-std LDB_PROCESS, GET_DOCUMENTS, SCROLLING_IN_TABLE).

For GP_HKTID='MZN01': GR_SAKNR_OI = {0001194424} (not empty) — so J_DAVANE's ECO09/MZN01 input ALONE does not hit the empty-fallback. Either (a) she entered a different HBKID/HKTID, (b) secondary timeout path (PROC_SET_CORRESPONDANCE LOOP at :1553+ reading PAYR), or (c) GJAHR=current-year fallback volume. Class-generalization scan of the full Y-stack found Claim 53 (empty-RANGE → unbounded-LDB-scan) to be UNIQUE to YTBAE002. YTBAE001 + YTBAE001_HR use a completely different selection mechanism: PERFORM 01_SELECT_BSIS in include YTBAM003 iterates TSAKO config-table (YTBAM003.abap:53-59) and is safe-by-construction (empty TSAKO → zero SELECTs, no unbounded scan). YFI_BANK_RECONCILIATION (2023) delegates to YCL_FI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_BL whose selection is user-controlled via S_HKONT SELECT-OPTION (YFI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_SEL.abap:6) — different design entirely. Therefore Claim 53 does NOT auto-extend to the rest of the family.

Additionally, live BSIS probe on 0001194424 returned only 13 rows, which cannot TIME_OUT on volume alone. Decisive evidence: user reproduced the program in ~2 seconds from HQ (fast LAN) with no dump. The network axis isolates the real mechanism (MODE 'E' BDC — see Claim 54). The defect at :1366-1370 is REAL but LATENT (never triggered J_DAVANE's dump). Kept as preventive-audit class.

claims.claim: CALL TRANSACTION ... USING <bdc> MODE 'E' inside a GET/LOOP over large bank-reconciliation data is NETWORK-COUPLED: any BDC error opens SAPGUI on the user's workstation and the cumulative GUI round-trip on slow WAN paths (UNESCO field offices) exceeds rdisp/max_wprun_time, producing a TIME_OUT in the caller's fetch loop with a stack that blames the LDB rather than the CALL TRANSACTION. Observed at YTBAE002.abap:27 (CONSTANTS: GC_MOD TYPE C VALUE 'E') + four CALL TRANSACTION sites inside the GET bsis / LDB_PROCESS loop: :723 (FB08 reverse), :771 (F-04 post-with-clearing), :819 (FBRA reset cleared), :853 (FB08 reverse alt branch) — all passing MODE GC_MOD and already collecting messages into Y_MESSTAB / GT_RECONCIL_MESS. Canonical fix: change MODE from 'E' to 'N' (silent). Errors remain visible in the captured Y_MESSTAB → GT_RECONCIL_MESS log, no functional regression. One-char fix file written at Zagentexecution/fixes/INC-000006906/YTBAE002_fix.abap.

evidence_for.cite: Line 27: CONSTANTS: GC_MOD TYPE C VALUE 'E'. Lines 723, 771, 819, 853: CALL TRANSACTION 'FB08'/'F-04'/'FBRA'/'FB08' USING BDCDTAB MODE GC_MOD MESSAGES INTO Y_MESSTAB. All four inside the GET bsis/LDB_PROCESS iteration body. Lines 754, 795, 840, 874: PROC_RECONCIL_MESS_ADD persists Y_MESSTAB to GT_RECONCIL_MESS (silent-capture path already present).

Same program, same user inputs, same data — from HQ (fast LAN) completes in ~2 seconds with no dump. From Maputo (slow WAN) TIME_OUTs. Only variable: network path. Isolates network handshake, not code or data, as the critical axis.

Three-path A/B/C test confirmed by user: (A) VPN path — no error. (B) Paris HQ network — no error. (C) Mozambique direct — TIME_OUT. Binary outcome correlates 1:1 with network path. Same program, same data, same SAP server. Promotes tier from TIER_2 (code + single reproduction) to TIER_1 (code + three independent network-path observations with identical cause/effect).

ST22 dump 2026-04-20 13:51:49 J_DAVANE P01 client 350. Runtime error TIME_OUT at SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS. SAP-note hint in the dump misleads — the consumed clock is in the BDC GUI handshake, not in PUT_BSIS's SELECT.

evidence_for.ref: SAP ABAP CALL TRANSACTION USING reference — MODE parameter
evidence_for.cite: MODE 'E' (Errors only) — 'opens dialog when BDC step raises an error'; MODE 'N' (no display) — 'silent, no SAPGUI interaction'. The dialog handshake is always client-side when 'E' fires.

YTBAE001.abap:118 declares C_MOD TYPE C VALUE 'E'. YTBAE001 includes YTBAM002 at :530; YTBAM002's 4 CALL TRANSACTION sites (:214, 262, 310, 344) inherit that C_MOD. YTBAE001_HR.abap:122 declares C_MOD='E'; YTBAE001_HR includes YTBAM002_HR at :567; YTBAM002_HR's 4 CALL TRANSACTION sites (:228, 276, 324, 358) explicitly pass MODE C_MOD. Total: 3 executables (YTBAE001, YTBAE001_HR, YTBAE002) in the bank-reconciliation Y-stack carry the MODE 'E' + CALL TRANSACTION pattern. Of those, ONLY YTBAE002 is ACTIVE (5 scheduled runs Mar 2026, dialog dump 2026-04-20). YTBAE001 has 1 TBTCO entry with STATUS=NULL and no runtime; YTBAE001_HR has zero TBTCO entries. Recommendation: fix YTBAE002 now (transport pending), defer YTBAE001/_HR behind a TCODE YTR1/YTR2/YTR2_HR usage review (KU-2026-057-02).

claims.claim: BCM routing at UNESCO is triggered by tcode OBPM5 configuration (table TFIBLMPAYBLOCK, 2 rows): LAUFK=' '/P + LAUFI='*' + XBATCH='X' — wildcard routes EVERY F110 and Payroll run into BCM evaluation. Downstream filters reduce actual BCM batch creation: (1) PM must be DMEE-enabled (T042Z.XBKKT='X' — 100% clean cut: no XBKKT='' ever creates a BCM batch), (2) co code ICTP is structurally excluded (0/603 runs), (3) BCM grouping rules (IMG > FSCM > BCM > Payment Grouping > Rule Maintenance) evaluate and assign one of 14 UNESCO-custom RULE_IDs (UNES_AP_ST, UNES_TR_TR, PAYROLL, IIEP_AP_ST, UIS_AP_MAX, UBO_AP_MAX, UIL_AP_ST, UNES_AP_10/11/EX/IK, UNES_AR_BP, UBO_AP_ST, UIS_AP_ST). The observation 'LAUFI ending in B ↔ BCM batch' (100% correlation) is a SYMPTOM of UNESCO naming convention for BCM-destined F110 variants combined with the OBPM5 wildcard match, NOT a SAP routing rule. Supersedes legacy brain memory claim 'LAUFI prefix B=BCM, 0/T/M=Direct' (falsified: prefix-B runs have 0% BCM linkage; prefix-0 runs with suffix-B have 100%).

evidence_for.cite: BNK_BATCH_HEADER: 27,443 batches 2014-2026, 100% have LAUFI ending in B. REGUH (full schema extract 15mo, 505,636 rows, 9,163 distinct runs): XBKKT='' → 0/2,941 BCM (100% clean negative), XBKKT='X' → 1,045/6,222 BCM (16.8%). Co code ICTP: 0/603 BCM. Companies UNES/IIEP/UIL/UBO/UIS: 10-29% BCM within XBKKT='X'.

Full config (2 rows only): {MANDT=350, LAUFK=' ', LAUFI='*', XBATCH='X'} and {MANDT=350, LAUFK='P', LAUFI='*', XBATCH='X'}. Stored in Zagentexecution/sap_data_extraction/extracted_data/payment_process_full/TFIBLMPAYBLOCK_full.json

https://sapinsider.org/sap-bank-communication-management-what-you-need-to-know/ — confirms OBPM5 identifier matching with BRM checkbox is the routing mechanism. 'Entering an asterisk in the field under the Identifier column and selecting the check box under the BRM column tells the system to send all payment runs to SAP Bank Communication Management.'

https://www.se80.co.uk/saptables/t/tfib/tfiblmpayblockid.htm — confirms TFIBLMPAYBLOCKID is 'Run Identification for Cross-Payment Run Payment Medium'. Tcode OBPM5 = 'Cross-Payment Run Payment Medium / Reservation for Cross-Payment Run Payment Media'.

Tested REGUV.X_WF_ACTIVE across 16,142 REGUH runs (28 months): FLAG IS NEVER SET (0 rows with 'X'). UNESCO does not use the SAP-standard workflow activation flag. BCM routing happens via OBPM5 wildcard config, not via REGUV per-run flag.

claims.resolution_notes: Session #60 deep-dive: superseded the legacy 'LAUFI prefix B=BCM' observation (a symptom) with the actual SAP routing rule via tcode OBPM5 (table TFIBLMPAYBLOCK). Evidence: 2 direct P01 config rows + 27,443 BCM batches with 100% consistency over 12 years + 505K REGUH rows with 100% clean XBKKT='' → no BCM negative cut. Full E2E chain documented in payment_bcm_companion.html node 7/10/11/18 tooltips. Remaining opacity: T74F_* tables (BCM grouping rule criteria) are pool/cluster, not RFC-readable — RULE_ID assignment mechanism inferred from 14 observed UNESCO-custom rule names but not from config.

claims.claim: SAP-standard CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_GENERIC::GENERIC_CALL populates structured address into FPAYHX_FREF buffers via explicit byte-offset layout: REF01[0..60]=ADRC.STREET, REF01[60..80]=BUILDING, REF01[80..90]=POST_CODE1, REF01[90..100]=REGION, REF01[100..110]=HOUSE_NUM1, REF01[110..116]=batch_booking, REF06[0..40]=CITY1. REF02 follows similar layout for Cdtr (POBox/HouseNo/Street/PostCd). This SAP-std mechanism activates at every Event 05 call regardless of country — the address mapping is universal.

evidence_for.cite: Inline ABAP comments document the byte layout; method calls cl_idfi_cgi_dmee_utils=>get_company_address with NATION param then writes substrings into cs_fpayhx_fref-ref01/06.

claims.resolution_notes: Discovered during expert agent re-evaluation. Critical: V001 DMEE trees just need to source structured nodes from FPAYHX-REF01/REF06 with MP_OFFSET — no UNESCO ABAP needed for the address mapping itself.

claims.claim: CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_FACTORY::GET_INSTANCE dispatches by country resolved from is_fpayhx-formi → format_properties.land1 → fpayhx-ubiso → fpayhx-ubnks. Builds class name as 'CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_' + country_key. Falls back to GENERIC if country class missing. Special case LI → CH. The country class is INSTANTIATED but the GENERIC instance is also created and runs (mo_instance = generic, ro_instance = country class). Country class only ADDS to FPAYHX_FREF, doesn't replace GENERIC's address mapping.

evidence_for.cite: Method get_instance lines 11-67 — dispatch logic with country fallback chain.

claims.resolution_notes: Verifies SAP std factory pattern. Closes uncertainty around Event 05 dispatch.

claims.claim: SAP-std country classes CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_FR/DE/IT/GB do NOT override the address mapping in GENERIC. They only ADD country-specific REF14 values: FR writes SIRET (16 chars from SAPFR1 PPC param), DE writes SUN+SeqTp from T042M/T042N, IT writes CUP/CIG/MGO from BSEG, GB writes T042M-bnkid+SeqTp. None touch REF01/REF02/REF06 (the address fields).

evidence_for.cite: FR class country_specific_call only writes cs_fpayhx_fref-ref14(16) = SIRET
IT class only writes ref14 with CUP/CIG/MGO concatenations
GB class only writes ref14[0..6]=bnkid and ref14[6..10]=SeqTp

claims.resolution_notes: Confirms SEPA structured address can be implemented via configuration only (DMEE tree + Event 05 registration of FI_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_CGI_05). No country-class dev needed.

claims.claim: UNESCO PPC (Payment Properties Customizing) tables YTFI_PPC_TAG (11 rows) + YTFI_PPC_STRUC (133 rows) exist in P01 as Z-tables. Schema: LAND1+TAG_ID+DEB_CRE+TAG_FULL (TAG) joined to LAND1+TAG_ID+PAY_TYPE+CODE_ORD+PPC_CODE+PPC_VALUE+PAY_STRUC+PAY_FIELD (STRUC). Distinct LAND1 values populated: AE(2), BH(1), CN(2), ID(1), IN(1), JO(1), MA(1), MY(1), PH(1). NO rows for FR/DE/IT/GB/US/BR — i.e., none of our 4 target trees' main countries. PPC dispatcher YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_FR is technically a PPC reader but for FR returns empty (no PPC rows for FR), so de-facto no-op.

evidence_for.cite: Total counts + LAND1 distribution probed.
Constructor SELECTs from ytfi_ppc_tag JOIN ytfi_ppc_struc into mt_ppc_cus.
FR get_value method delegates to UTIL get_tag_value_from_custo (PPC dispatch).

claims.resolution_notes: Initial agent concern that PPC tables would need V001 cleanup is resolved: tables are populated only for non-target Asian/Middle East/Africa countries. Our target countries (FR/DE/IT/US/CA/BR/etc.) have zero PPC rows, so FR/DE/IT BAdI classes are effective no-ops for our trees.

claims.claim: Surgical change scope per component (Phase 2 V001 plan) — agent re-evaluation result: (a) /SEPA_CT_UNES: V001 = pure DMEE tree node additions sourcing FPAYHX-REF01[0/60/80/90/100] + REF06[0..40], plus 1 row in TFPM042FB to register Event 05=FI_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_CGI_05. ZERO ABAP. (b) /CGI_XML_CT_UNESCO + _1: V001 = fix CdtrAgt unstructured nodes only, source from BNKA standard bank DB. ZERO ABAP. (c) /CITI/XML/UNESCO/DC_V3_01: BLOCKED — must extract /CITIPMW/V3_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_05 source first (was not extracted via RFC). User's 'surgical' instinct correct.

claims.claim: CITIPMW V3 Event 05 FM /CITIPMW/V3_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_05 (PNAME=/CITIPMW/SAPLPMWV3, INCLUDE=31) populates FPAYHX_FREF address using IDENTICAL byte layout as SAP-std CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_GENERIC: REF01[0..60]=ADRC.STREET, REF01[60..80]=BUILDING, REF01[80..90]=POST_CODE1, REF01[90..100]=REGION, REF01[100..110]=HOUSE_NUM1, REF06[0..40]=CITY1. known_unknowns.question: Why is the IF bseg_xref1 = space guard commented out in YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:998? When was it removed, by whom, and why?
known_unknowns.why_unknown: No git blame on YRGGBS00 source. Only the SROCHA 2010 comment marker visible elsewhere in the file.
known_unknowns.follow_up: Check TADIR / E071 transport history for YRGGBS00 changes. Or ask UNESCO ABAP team directly.
known_unknowns.notes: YRGGBS00 version history via SE38 version comparison. H48 scope.
known_unknowns.answer_partial: Confirmed via code read (YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:998): line `* if bseg_xref1 = space.` IS commented out. UXR1 therefore unconditionally overwrites bseg_xref1 with USR05.Y_USERFO whenever the user has that param, silently losing any user-typed value. ASYMMETRY DISCOVERED: UXR2 (line 1026) STILL has the guard `IF bseg_xref2 = space` — only UXR1 is unguarded. This is the root cause of manual XREF drift observed in DQ-019 (21,754 Q1 2026 edits).
known_unknowns.still_unknown: Exact transport that commented the IF bseg_xref1 = space guard. Narrowed to I_KONAKOV transports 2009-2010. To definitively close: SE38 diff between D01K951442 and its predecessor D01K951407 (also KONAKOV, 2009-08-27 different hour) on lines 996-1016.
known_unknowns.answer_update: Transport history via E071 + E070 extracted: 57 transports modified YRGGBS00 between 2002-2026. Main editor: I_KONAKOV (~20 transports spanning 2008-2023, including the 2015-2017 era when substitution mods were heavy). Other editors: MAGAL/PARENT-DU (2002 initial), AHOUNOU (2003-2006), MENARD (2022-2026), RIOS (2025). CLUSTER of substitution-related transports: D01K951442 (2009-08-27 'WF - FI substitutions user-exit correction' by KONAKOV), D01K951618 (2009-08-27 'WF - FI Substitution rules' by KONAKOV), D01K960319 (2010-04-26 'WF - FI substitutions' by KONAKOV). The guard was most likely commented in one of these KONAKOV transports. Definitive identification requires diff-by-diff compare of YRGGBS00 source between transport versions (VRSD returned TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA via RFC — would need SE38 Version Management GUI).

known_unknowns.question: Where is the substitution step-to-prerequisite linkage stored at UNESCO? GB905 and GB921 are both empty via RFC_READ_TABLE broad probe.
known_unknowns.why_unknown: Standard SAP GGB1 expects step prerequisites in GB905. They are empty at UNESCO P01 (Session #051 probe). Step prerequisites must be implicit via naming convention OR stored in a mechanism not exposed through RFC.
known_unknowns.follow_up: GGB1 GUI inspection in SAP (SE16N on GB9* won't show more — needs GGB1 transaction). Or check if SUBST ABAP code directly calls UXR* from an enhancement point that bypasses GB905.
known_unknowns.notes: Check GB01 / GGB1 tables via RFC + GCTAB/GCSTR secondary tables. H48 scope.
known_unknowns.answer: Substitution step prerequisites at UNESCO ARE DECLARATIVE via 3-table chain: GB922 (step definition: SUBSTID+SUBSEQNR+CONDID implicit via exit name) -> GB921 (step-to-CONDID linkage: 16 rows for SUBSTID='UNESCO') -> GB901 (boolean expressions indexed by CONDID, 583 rows total, 56 rows for BOOLID LIKE '3UNESCO%'). My prior session #054 claim that prerequisites were 'procedural in FORM bodies' was WRONG — they are declarative. Correction: the FORM body contains the ACTION logic once the declarative prereq passes. KEY FINDING: step 005 (UXR1/XREF1) has NO prerequisite row in GB901 — fires UNCONDITIONALLY for every BSEG line. Step 006 (UXR2/XREF2) has a prerequisite: `HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021')` — only fires for specific GL accounts. GB905 exists but returned 0 rows for UNES-related BOOLIDs.
known_unknowns.findings_location: Gold DB: GB922 (17 UNES rows), GB901 (583 rows with BOOLEXP), GB903 (3 rows); YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt FORM bodies
known_unknowns.tables_architecture: GB922 = step definition. GB921 = step-to-condition linkage (MANDT+SUBSTID+SUBSEQNR -> CONDID). GB901 = boolean expression body (BOOLID+SEQNR -> BOOLEXP multi-line text). GB903 = boolean header (BOOLID -> SHORTNAME+SETID). GB931 = VALIDATION step-to-condition for VALID='UNES' (12 rows, separate from substitutions). GB02C = message class mapping.

known_unknowns.question: Why does F110 substitution fire on the vendor clearing line but NOT on the bank GL line, while F-53 fires on both? Empirically observed asymmetry with unknown mechanism.
known_unknowns.impact: MEDIUM - explains F-53 vs F110 asymmetry
known_unknowns.why_unknown: Could be (a) F110 uses BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST which bypasses callpoint 3, (b) a step-level filter on F110 BLART='ZP', (c) F110 payment method program creates the bank GL line post-substitution. Without GB905 or GGB1 GUI access, can't distinguish.
known_unknowns.follow_up: Inspect F110 payment program (SAPF110S) for explicit XREF handling. Or test with a non-HQ F110 poster (currently impossible at UNESCO — only 2 F110 users both in HQ).
known_unknowns.notes: Empirical ST05 trace of F110 vs F-53 substitution callpoint firing in TS3. Requires live test posting. H48 scope.
known_unknowns.scope: UXR1 has NO filter by TCODE/BUKRS/callpoint context within the FORM body. Both UXR1 and UXR2 are registered as callpoint-3 (complete document) substitutions for UNES. Therefore the F110 vs F-53 asymmetry cannot come from the FORM itself. Two remaining hypotheses: (a) F110 batch context iterates BSEG lines differently, applying substitution only to vendor lines (SAP standard behavior with bseg-lifnr filter inside the callpoint dispatcher), (b) GGB1 or GB921 callpoint-level prerequisites filter tcode but are not extracted to Gold DB. Definitive answer needs ST05 SQL trace in TS3 with one F110 run + one F-53 posting to capture the actual substitution call sequence per BSEG line.
known_unknowns.blocks_on: Live SAP session in TS3 — user-assisted work required. Cannot proceed autonomously via RFC.
known_unknowns.enrichment: New data reduces the asymmetry mystery but does not fully explain F110 vs F-53. Finding: UXR1 has NO declarative prereq (fires on every BSEG line of every UNESCO posting). UXR2 has declarative prereq HKONT IN (bank-related GL list). So XREF1 auto-write should fire for EVERY line in EVERY tcode (F110, F-53, FB60, MIRO, etc.). The asymmetry observed in INC-000005240 (F110 fires on vendor clearing line but not bank GL line) must therefore come from either (a) SAP standard F110 batch context only iterating certain lines through callpoint 3, or (b) a downstream filter on bseg-hkont within substitution dispatcher. ST05 trace still required to resolve.

known_unknowns.question: Does UIS have bank accounts besides Citibank Canada (USD 2017588014, CAD 2017588006)? If yes, are Ophelia STEPHENSON-ODLE (PERNR 10136066) and Yanhong ZHANG (PERNR 10098989) authorized signatories on any of those other cartons? They are currently active in SAP RY 50010054 (UIS signatures for all transfers) but not on the 2026-04-02 Citibank Canada carton.
known_unknowns.why_unknown: SAP BCM responsibility groups are entity-level, not bank-account-level. One group 'UIS signatures for all transfers' covers every UIS bank account at every bank. We only have the Citibank Canada carton on file. Without all UIS cartons we cannot distinguish legitimate multi-bank coverage from stale SAP config.

known_unknowns.question: Is the role split on UIS BCM rules intentional? Anssi YLI-HIETANEN (10097358, Treasurer) is on rule 90000004 (Commit) but not on rule 90000005 (Validation). Lamin SANNEH (10150918) is on rule 90000005 but not on rule 90000004. The Citibank Canada carton lists both flatly as 'authorized to sign jointly two by two' without distinguishing validator vs committer. Is the SAP split deliberate (e.g., Treasurer commits only) or a maintenance accident?
known_unknowns.why_unknown: The carton does not carry SAP rule semantics. The business rule for the validator/committer split is oral/undocumented and must be confirmed by TRS.
known_unknowns.blocks_action: Any role-mirroring cleanup on UIS BCM panel
known_unknowns.answer: The role split was a maintenance drift, NOT intentional. TRS confirmed by authorizing the full cleanup on 2026-04-13: all 8 signatories on the Citibank Canada carton are now on BOTH rule 90000004 (Commit) and rule 90000005 (Validation). 'Sign jointly two by two' means any two authorized signatories can sign together — no validator-only or committer-only split by design. Sanneh was added to Commit, Yli-Hietanen was added to Validation.
known_unknowns.notes: ANSWERED session #052 (see `answered_session` field). Role split is intentional per OOCU_RESP structure.

user_questions.question: Can we consolidate the 30+ individual feedback memory files into one structured file while preserving knowledge?

user_questions.answer: Migrated irreplaceable knowledge (feedback rules, project state docs) into brain_v2/agent_rules/ and knowledge/ inside the project. ~/.claude/memory/ is now a cache, not source of truth.

user_questions.answer: Yes — copied plan to session_plans/04092026_session049_brain_v3_hybrid_rebuild.md and added feedback rule #47 to always do this immediately after plan mode.

user_questions.question: Will the brain_state.json approach scale when context window doubles to 2M+ tokens?
user_questions.answer: Yes — and we should be LESS conservative about loading, not more. Saved as feedback rule #49. Design for growing context.

user_questions.question: Should we design brain architecture using human information patterns (one-file-per-entity)?
user_questions.answer: NEVER. Saved as feedback rule #48 (CRITICAL). Agent thinks in 1 dense file, not 50 scattered files. Deleted the 108 index/*.md files we initially created.

data_quality.issue: Vendor account group (LFA1.KTOKK) and reconciliation account (LFB1.AKONT) drift from the canonical mapping in T077K. Discovered case: vendor 10133079 (Katja HINZ) has KTOKK=SCSA â†’ AKONT=2021011 in IIEP company code, while T077K canonical mapping for SCSA staff expects AKONT=2021010 (and the GGB1 rule set only covers 2021042 for KTOKK=UNES). Class of defect, not a one-off â€” full vendor master never audited for KTOKKâ†”AKONT consistency.
data_quality.impact: Caused INC-000006073 (Travel posting RW609/ZFI020 for cross-funded IIEPâ†”UNES trips). Class effect: any vendor with non-canonical AKONT silently passes posting until an intercompany scenario or new GGB1 rule set exposes the gap. UNESCO has 316K vendors in LFA1; total exposure unquantified.
data_quality.workaround: (a) Run vendor_master_integrity_check.py against Gold DB to list all LFB1 rows where AKONT does not match T077K canonical mapping for the LFA1.KTOKK. (b) For each violation, decide whether to correct AKONT (FK02) or extend GGB1 substitution (YFI_BASU_MOD). (c) Promote this to a recurring monthly check.

data_quality.resolution_path: Documented as extraction pattern: always extract transport contents BEFORE release. Previous extraction is source of truth.

incidents.title: PRRW Travel posting RW609 + ZFI020 for IIEP traveler on UNES-funded trip

error_messages.text: For IIEP company code, only use business area PAR, IBA or FEL

incidents.root_cause_summary: Two independent factors converged: (1) NEW intercompany trip — first time the employee's UNES-fund posting created a cross-company-code document, exposing that LHRTSF01.abap:852 only fills GSBER for same-company lines; (2) OLD vendor master defect — vendor created in 2016 with KTOKK=SCSA → LFB1.AKONT=2021011 in IIEP, which is the only AKONT not covered by GGB1 substitution rule 3IIEP###002 (which covers 2021042/2021061). All three fallback safety nets are independently broken: BAdI PA0027-02 expired 2021-01-31, ZXTRVU05 inst_fund_ba_wbs_cc commented out for non-UNES, GGB1 rule has no entry for HKONT=2021011.
code_validation_chain: LHRTSF01.abap:852 IF epk-bukst = epk-bukrs --> NO MATCH (IIEP <> UNES) --> GSBER stays empty
LHRTSF01.abap:1693 CLEAR epk-gsber on vendor lines (koart=K, ktosl=HRP)
ZCL_IM_TRIP_POST_FI_CM00A.abap:12-37 reads PA0027-02 --> expired 2021 --> CHECK sy-subrc exits
ZXTRVU05.abap:63 WHEN OTHERS --> inst_fund_ba_wbs_cc COMMENTED OUT
LHRTSU05.abap:155 BAPI_ACC_EMPLOYEE_PAY_POST returns error --> wrap as RW609 + ZFI020 text

fix_path.immediate: PA30 — Extend PA0027 subtype 02 for vendor 10133079 (IIEP/PAR, current dates) — unblocks Rita today
fix_path.structural: FK02 — Change LFA1.KTOKK from SCSA to UNES for vendor 10133079 (corrects LFB1.AKONT IIEP from 2021011 to 2021042 which is GGB1-covered)
preventive: YFI_BASU_MOD — Add HKONT=2021011 to YTFI_BA_SUBST for IIEP (no transport) so any future SCSA-GL exposure is auto-substituted
GGB1 — Add HKONT=2021011 to substitution rule 3IIEP###002 (transported)
ZXTRVU05 — Uncomment inst_fund_ba_wbs_cc in WHEN OTHERS branch (transported, requires testing)
Run vendor_master_integrity_check.py monthly to surface any other vendor with outlier AKONT vs same-KTOKK peers

related_claims.claim: VENDOR account group KTOKK=SCSA uses AKONT=2021011 which has NO GGB1 substitution rule for business area
Travel advance posting requires BUKRS=BUKST for GSBER propagation in LHRTSF01

evidence_extracted_this_incident.code_extracted: 34 SAP standard files (24,559 lines) in extracted_code/SAP_STANDARD/TV_TRAVEL/

lessons: When an incident exposes a NEW domain (Travel), create a dedicated knowledge/domains/<DOMAIN>/ folder before closing.

Always cross-check the configuration source-of-truth table. We checked YTFI_BA_SUBST (the user-friendly maintenance persistence) but the real GGB1 rules live in GB901/GB922 — which had to be extracted mid-incident.
An invisible 10-year defect can surface from a single new combination of inputs (employee fund change + intercompany scenario).
open_followups: Quantify MGIE/ICBA exposure (KU-017) — both have travelers using GL 2021011 in same-company mode today
Confirm with Travel/HR team whether intercompany was intentional in trip 0101004544 or a user error
Audit PA0027 subtype 02 expirations across all employees (BAdI safety net broken systemically?)

incidents.title: F-53 manual payment writes XREF1/XREF2='HQ' instead of 'JAK' for Jakarta user
incidents.primary_subject: AL_JONATHAN USR05.Y_USERFO='HQ' → XREF tagging wrong on all his F-53 postings
incidents.root_cause_summary: AL_JONATHAN's USR05 SU3 parameter Y_USERFO='HQ' instead of 'JAK'. YRGGBS00 form UXR1 (line 996) writes bseg_xref1 = usr05-parva unconditionally (guard commented out). UXR2 (line 1024) writes bseg_xref2 = usr05-parva when XREF2 is blank (always true on F-53 since the screen hides the field). Both run at callpoint 3 under GB922 SUBSTID='UNESCO' steps 005/006 with empty GB905 prerequisites (fires unconditionally on every BSEG line). The code works as designed; the defect is master data only. Independent confirmation via A_HIZKIA (also Jakarta, Y_USERFO='JAK' verified) manually correcting his docs via FBL3N since 2026-02-16. End-to-end gap: VALID='UNES' (12 steps in GB931) has ZERO steps that check XREF fields; F-53/FBZ2 is in ZERO validation prerequisites; 21,754 manual FBL3N/FBL1N/FB02 corrections in Q1 2026 on UNES BELEG by 242 users show this is systemic.
code_validation_chain: YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:230 exits-name='UXR1' (substitution exit registered)
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:235 exits-name='UXR2' (substitution exit registered)
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:996 FORM uxr1: SELECT usr05 WHERE parid='Y_USERFO' → bseg_xref1 = usr05-parva (guard IF bseg_xref1=space commented out at line 998 — always overwrites)
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1007 SELECT yfo_codes WHERE focod=bseg_xref1 → MESSAGE w018(zfi) warning if missing (non-blocking)
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1024 FORM uxr2: IF bseg_xref2=space → SELECT usr05 Y_USERFO → bseg_xref2 = usr05-parva (no validation in auto-write branch)
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1035 ELSE branch (user-typed XREF2) → SELECT yfo_codes → MESSAGE e018(zfi) hard error if missing
YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1050-1119 FORM uzls: derives bseg_zlsch from bseg_xref2 per company code
Live RFC 2026-04-09: USR05 WHERE BNAME='AL_JONATHAN' AND PARID='Y_USERFO' → PARVA='HQ'
Live RFC 2026-04-09: PA0001 WHERE PERNR=10175236 → WERKS='ID00' BTRTL='JKT' (HR master says Jakarta)
Live RFC 2026-04-09: BSAK for 10 of his FBZ2 docs → ALL have XREF1='HQ' XREF2='HQ'
CDPOS 3100003438/2026 CHANGENR=0118205716: XREF1 'HQ'→'JAK' by A_HIZKIA via FBL3N on 2026-02-16 — proves substitution fired at posting time (VALUE_OLD='HQ')
CDPOS 3100003439/2026 CHANGENR=0118205748: same pattern
RFC extracted GB93 (17 rows) + GB931 (12 steps for VALID='UNES') on 2026-04-09: confirms F-53/FBZ2 in ZERO validation prerequisites
A_HIZKIA USR05 Y_USERFO='JAK' verified — independent confirmation of correct target value

fix_path.blocking_prerequisite: Verify 'JAK' exists in YFO_CODES.FOCOD via SE16N. If not, add row via SM30 BEFORE SU3 change. Otherwise w018 warning fires on every posting and e018 hard error if user ever types 'JAK' manually (KU-027).
fix_path.tactical: SU3 / SU01 → AL_JONATHAN → Parameters → Y_USERFO: 'HQ' → 'JAK'. Smoke test with a test F-53 posting. Verify ZLSCH downstream behavior (will become 'O' for JAK — confirm with Jakarta treasury).
fix_path.strategic_master_data: Run PA0001×PA0105×USR05 audit query (knowledge/domains/Treasury/xref_office_tagging_model.md §7.2) to enumerate all AL_JONATHAN-class drifted users. Batch-correct.
fix_path.strategic_validation: Add 13th step to VALID='UNES' that checks BSEG-XREF1 against HR-derived expected office (see xref_office_tagging_model.md §7.3).
fix_path.strategic_code: Close UXR1 overwrite gap (re-enable IF bseg_xref1=space guard at line 998) — separate transport.

evidence_extracted_this_incident: CDPOS (2 A_HIZKIA FBL3N changes with XREF1 HQ→JAK proof)
CDHDR (full BELEG change scan for AL_JONATHAN window)
GB931 (12 rows for VALID='UNES' — full validation step map)

incidents.title: Budget Rate custom solution allows cross-currency consumption against EUR-reserved FR -> AVC pool drift -> FMAVC005
incidents.reporter: Laetitia Vonthron (adm.cad) -> Christina Lopez-Chemouny -> Yimiao Guo -> Illya Konakov -> Pablo Lopez
incidents.primary_subject: FR 3250117351 line 39 (EUR carryforward to 2026, fund 3110111021 / FUNDSCTR PAX / CMMTITEM TC); EqG ticket reimbursement for SG Bivini Mangue
error_messages: FMAVC005 Annual budget exceeded by 0,87 USD (FM Availability Control) for document item 00002
incidents.root_cause_summary: 15-member ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE composite has gate-input asymmetry: Camp A (FR-anchored: CHECK_CONS, EF_FUND_RESERVATION, FUNDBLOCK) always fires for EUR FRs; Camp B (posting-anchored: PAYCOMMIT, NEW_ITEM, AVC, KBLEW, FI) silently skips when posting currency is not EUR. FR persisted at BR (1.09529); consumption persisted at identity. AVC pool drifts fund-by-fund until cover-group hits zero headroom. Vonthron's fund 3110111021/PAX/TC has $12,897.80 budget exactly matched by $12,897.80 of carryforward FR commitments -> Available=0 -> any new USD posting fires FMAVC005.
code_validation_chain: extracted_code/CLAS/YCL_FM_BR_EXCHANGE_RATE_BL/CM00A line 8: APPEND 'EUR' to mr_waers (the perimeter line)
extracted_code/CLAS/YCL_FM_BR_EXCHANGE_RATE_BL/CM004: check_conditions exits abap_false if iv_waers NOT IN mr_waers
ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_CHECK_CONS Enh1 (Camp A): iv_waers = m_r_doc->m_f_kblk-waers (FR header EUR) -> PASS -> recalc -> Enh2 RESTORE original (in-memory only, no persistence)
ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_NEW_ITEM (Camp B): iv_waers = c_f_fmoi-twaer (USD for USD posting) -> FAIL -> no recalc
ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_AVC Enh1+3 (Camp B): iv_waers = <ls_fmioi>-twaer / <ls_fmifiit>-twaer per-row -> mixed, USD rows skip
ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_KBLEW (Camp B): iv_waers = ls_tr_kblew-waers (USD) -> FAIL -> no recalc
ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_FI (Camp B): iv_waers = <ls_fmifiit>-twaer (USD) -> FAIL -> no recalc + no audit + mt_avc_fund not appended -> fmavc_reinit_on_event handler never registered
FMAVCT(RLDNR=9H,RFIKRS=UNES,RYEAR=2026,RFUND=3110111021,RFUNDSCTR=PAX,RCMMTITEM=TC) ALLOCTYPE_9=KBFC HSL01=12,897.80 USD
Open FR commitments same key: 5 lines (RFPOS 38/39/40/43/45 of FR 3250117351) summing to $12,897.80
Available = 12,897.80 - 12,897.80 = 0 -> FMAVC005 fires on any positive posting

fix_path.immediate_workaround: Post the EqG invoice in EUR (validated by Konakov 2026-03-30 'when I try to post the invoice in EUR instead of USD it works fine'). Use XOF->EUR rate at translation date.

All 10 steps verifiable against extracted source or Gold DB.

incidents.root_cause_mechanism: PUT_BSIS SELECT on table BSIS ran past rdisp/max_wprun_time via a non-selective access path. Two drivers: (1) HKONT scope possibly resolved from a YBANK_* set; J_DAVANEs primary GL 0001094424 (ECO09 MZN) is NOT in any YBANK_* set (233 of 383 distinct T012K HKONTs uncovered, 61 percent); empty set expansion degrades SELECT to BUKRS-only scan = 2,005,304 BSIS rows for UNES. (2) BUDAT / AUGDT range open combined with (1). SAP-note text embedded in the dump explicitly names access without suitable index and result set too large. Batch executions of YTBAE002 succeeded 5 times in March 2026 (TBTCO status F) because batch has no dispatcher time cap -- dialog vs batch is the trigger, not UBA01 config churn.

network_path_reproduction.cite: User-confirmed three-path A/B/C test 2026-04-20. Binary outcome correlates 1:1 with network path. Same program, same data, same P01 server. Promotes root_cause tier from TIER_2 (code + single reproduction) to TIER_1 (code + multi-path empirical).

incidents.source_extracted_method: pyrfc SNC/SSO on D01 via RPY_PROGRAM_READ (D01 ADT HTTP Basic blocked — password locked)
incidents.root_cause_mechanism_detail: YTBAE002.abap:27 declares CONSTANTS: GC_MOD TYPE C VALUE 'E' (SAP CALL TRANSACTION mode 'Display errors only, opens SAPGUI on BDC error'). The program issues four CALL TRANSACTION ... USING BDCDTAB MODE GC_MOD MESSAGES INTO Y_MESSTAB inside the GET bsis / LDB_PROCESS iteration body: YTBAE002.abap:723 (FB08 reverse), :771 (F-04 post-with-clearing), :819 (FBRA reset cleared), :853 (FB08 second branch). On slow WAN paths (Maputo field office) the cumulative GUI round-trip per BDC error exceeds rdisp/max_wprun_time → dispatcher aborts with TIME_OUT at whatever SELECT the work-process is waiting in (SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS in this case). Errors are already captured in Y_MESSTAB at each call site and persisted by PROC_RECONCIL_MESS_ADD at :754/:795/:840/:874 into GT_RECONCIL_MESS for the list output — so silent capture is already wired. Fix: change line 27 'E' → 'N'.
incidents.root_cause_code_anchor: YTBAE002.abap:27 (primary) + YTBAE002.abap:723, :771, :819, :853 (the four CALL TRANSACTION sites)

Claim 53 stays active as LATENT defect — real code pattern bug but did not trigger this dump.

The program plus class YCL_FI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_BL were built 2023-04 through 2023-06 by external consultant N_MENARD in 10 transports and have had ZERO changes since 2023-06-29 -- so this is a latent data-driven defect, not a regression. Rank-2: TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED during PREPARE_DETAILED_DATA against 686 uncleared BSIS items on GL 0001022424. Rank-3: CONVT_NO_NUMBER if WAERS=MZM (deprecated) on BST02 is touched. Rank-4: OBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED in HANDLE_USER_COMMAND on empty ALV. SECONDARY (demoted): UBA01 / YBANK_ACCOUNTS_FO_OTH set transport collateral. domains_layer._design: Layer 14 â€” Domain registry with reverse indexes and 3-axis taxonomy. Every entity knows its domain; every domain knows its entities. Zero compression: sub-topics within a domain are preserved as first-class children, not flattened. Three axes per entity: functional (business), module (SAP module code), process (UNESCO process B2R/H2R/P2P/T2R/P2D). Established Session #056.

BCM.description: Bank Communication Management â€” payment batch orchestration, dual-control workflow (90000003 main, 90000004 BNK_COM, 90000005 BNK_INI), signatory/approver rules stored as PD objects OTYPE='RY' under PLVAR='01', exception-list governance (AE/JO/embargo).

dual_control_gap.description: 3,359 same-user batches 2024-2026 (CRUSR=CHUSR), $656M exposure. Root cause: structural understaffing of HQ treasury (only 2 operators: C_LOPEZ 94.7%, I_MARQUAND 92.9%). Not fraud â€” automation debt.

signatory_rules.description: Workflow responsibility groups 90000004 BNK_COM / 90000005 BNK_INI stored as PD objects (HRP1000/HRP1001) OTYPE='RY', PLVAR='01'. Every BCM spec for a new office or rule must include both RY STEXT and the OTYPE='RY' PLVAR='01' keys.

workflow_mod_path.description: Path 4 remediation â€” preventive workflow 90000003 modification to enforce CHUSR != CRUSR at SAP level. Blocked on YWFI package source extraction (H14).

Treasury.description: Treasury operations â€” house bank configuration, electronic bank statement (EBS) import and posting, payment execution (F110 + BCM), cash management. Integrates 9 company codes, 2 currencies, and 6+ house banks.

house_bank_configuration.description: 13 config steps for house bank create/modify/close, 15 compliance checks, cross-system D01â†”P01 comparison, ECO09 benchmark patterns. Tables: T012 (house bank), T012K (account ID), SKA1/SKB1/SKAT (G/L), T030H (posting keys), T035D (posting string), T018V (reason codes), T042I (bank clearing), TIBAN (IBAN), SETLEAF (hierarchy).

bank_statement_ebs.description: E2E bank statement processing: MT940 import, EBS posting (FEB_BSPROC), clearing, FEBAN reconciliation. 3 config tiers (basic/posting-rules/interpretation algorithms). Volumes: 223K FEBEP (statement items), 964K FEBRE (line items). Process mining: 263K events.

bank_reconciliation.description: Bank reconciliation program inventory + process model. Two tiers: (a) CENTRAL substitution chain YRGGBS00 XREF1/XREF2 (UXR1/UXR2 FORMs) with GB901 prerequisites; (b) FIELD-OFFICE custom Y-stack â€” 15 programs wrapping standard clearing TCODEs (FB08/F-04/FBRA) via BDC CALL TRANSACTION.

field_office_custom_clearing.description: UNESCO Y-stack of 15 custom bank-reconciliation programs used by FIELD OFFICES (UBO/FU sites like Maputo MZN, Brasilia BRA, Eco09) to orchestrate selection + clearing. Wrap standard FI clearing TCODEs (FB08, F-04, FBRA) via BDC CALL TRANSACTION. NOT a replacement â€” a thin UNESCO-specific wrapper adding: SKB1 company-code scoping, batch iteration via LDB SAPDBSDF, MZN/ECO/UBA company-code-specific logic, list-output error reporting. Discovered in depth via INC-000006906 (Maputo MZN TIME_OUT, Session #058).

xref_office_tagging.description: Office-code XREF1/XREF2 substitution model â€” YRGGBS00 overwrites XREF fields with office codes. Session #055 discovered DOUBLE ASYMMETRY: UXR1 has no GB901 prereq + commented-out guard (fires unconditionally); UXR2 has HKONT-in-6-bank-GLs prereq + active guard.

payment_landscape.description: Full payment landscape: F110 run â†’ BCM batch â†’ DMEE file â†’ bank. 1.4M process mining events, 550K cases, 9 company codes. 8 DMEE trees variations.

operational_tiers.central: HQ Treasury operations â€” YRGGBS00 substitution, F110, BCM dual-control 90000003, Paris

FI.description: Financial Accounting â€” GL, AP, AR, posting rules, validations/substitutions (GGB0/GGB1), BusA derivation. Bridges to PSM via FMIFIIT.KNBELNR=BKPF.BELNR.

validations_substitutions.description: GGB0 validations + GGB1 substitutions. OB28/OBBH assignment is OPTIONAL at UNESCO â€” 3 of 9 institutes (MGIE/ICBA/IBE) run without any. YRGGBS00 holds FORM bodies (UXR1/UXR2 etc.) + declarative prereqs in GB901.

gl_master_data.description: G/L account master: SKA1 (chart of accounts), SKB1 (company-code-specific), SKAT (text). Account comparison script detects wrong HBKID/KTOKS/XINTB; UPDATE via RFC_ABAP_INSTALL_AND_RUN.

busa_derivation.description: Business Area derivation logic for Travel postings. Intercompany scenario exposes LHRTSF01.abap:852 only fills GSBER for same-company lines.

HCM.description: Human Capital Management â€” employee lifecycle, 36 infotypes, payroll, benefits, Fiori apps (Offboarding, Asr Framework), OM, travel integration.

infotypes.description: 36 infotypes used by UNESCO HCM (PA0001-PA9999 range, PD-RY for workflow groups)

payroll.description: Payroll processing â€” Allos vs Y1 BDC pattern forensics

PSM.description: Public Sector Management / Fund Management â€” budget, commitments, FM-FI bridge via FMIFIIT, WRTTP filter, project cost recovery.

fm_fi_bridge.description: Bridge via FMIFIIT.KNBELNR=BKPF.BELNR; FM uses FKBTR (not HSL), PERIO (not POPER), FMBELNR (not BELNR)

Payment.description: Payment execution sub-domain of Treasury â€” F110, DMEE trees, 8 DMEE variations, BCM batch orchestration, 1.4M events process mining.

BusinessPartner.description: Vendor/customer/BP master data â€” SCSA vendor category, KTOKK â†’ AKONT mapping, intercompany BP scenarios.

Travel.description: Travel management â€” PRRW posting, PR02 trip create, BusA derivation, intercompany travel advance scenarios, PTRV tables.

Procurement.description: Procurement â€” PO release, entry sheets, GR. Enhancements analysis document.

Transport_Intelligence.description: SAP Transport Order intelligence â€” 7,745 transports classified across 21 modules. Pre-import risk analysis, companion generation, module-level taxonomy.

Integration.description: Interface landscape â€” 239 RFC destinations, 38 systems, 19.4K IDocs. DBCON (SQL Server: TULIP, UNESDIR), .NET Connector (SISTER, HR Workflow, CMT, UBO, Travel, Mouv, Procurement), MuleSoft (PROJECT02), BizTalk (SuccessFactors planned).

Output.description: Output determination â€” printing, forms, SmartForms, Adobe forms.

PS.description: Project System â€” WBS elements, project master, FM-PS bridge via PRPS.OBJNR â†’ POSID.

RE-FX.description: Real Estate Flexible â€” lease contracts, property master, RE-FX specific posting.

PY-Finance.description: PY-Finance — UNESCO non-standard domain where the Finance team (not HR Payroll) owns wage type configuration. Covers the 5 core wage type tables (T512T/W/T52DZ/T52EL/T52EZ), pay scale family (V_T510/V_T510_C/V_T510F_B/V_T510M/T511), custom schemas (PSCC 98100007-34) and PCRs (PCYC UN25/ZCSI/ZN31/ZN54/ZN60/ZOV0), plus country-level LCR monthly activities and Field Office salary scale revisions.

lcr_monthly.description: Local Cost Rate monthly activities per country. Owner: L_CABALLE. 106 transports/4y. Touches V_T510/T511. Naming: 'C - HR - PY : LCR_[Month] payroll activities[_Country]'.

salary_scale_fo.description: Field Office salary scale revisions for SC (Service Contract) staff. Owner: A_SEFIANI. 41 transports/4y. Per-country ad-hoc (Brasilia, India, Kyrgyzstan, Algeria, Mauritania, Nigeria, Guinea-Bissau, Solomon Islands, UNDP).

csi.description: Cost of Staff Index adjustments. Owner: A_SEFIANI. 17 transports/4y. End-of-year rollover. Uses custom cycle PCYC/ZCSI.

pensionable_remuneration.description: Annual pensionable remuneration updates for UNDP administrative and International Staff.

ticket_new_country_setup.description: Treasury team (FP_SPEZZANO/M_SPRONK) crossover: service desk ticket triggers full 5-table wage type chain setup for a new country/Field Office payment method. 6 transports/4y. Tickets: 133188 (UNDP 2022), 168627 (Sao Tome STN 2024), 172777 (Mongolia 2024), 180995 (Mozambique+Sri Lanka LKR 2024).

custom_schemas_pcrs.description: UNESCO-owned payroll schemas (PSCC 98100007-98100034) and PCRs (PCYC UN25, ZCSI, ZN31, ZN54, ZN60, ZOV0, ZPPF, ZPP0, ZPP1, ZUSD, ZUXX). Owner: N_MENARD.

HR-Workflows.description: HR Workflows - long-running UNESCO domain covering end-user-facing HR self-service flows: Offboarding, Benefits Enrollment, Family Management, Rental, Education Grant, Secondary Dependent. Built on SAPUI5 Fiori + WebDynpro ABAP + ASR Framework + ZCL_HR_FIORI_* handler classes. Activity 2017-2026. Strategic context: migration to SuccessFactors (SFSF) in progress, target completion 2026. End state = hybrid SFSF + retained UNESCO custom apps. The Core HR Project 2025-2026 burst IS the SFSF integration project.

staff_rejected_flow.description: STAFF REJECT at Step 01 -> STAFF_REJECTED status -> creator revises + RESUBMITs (fresh Step 01) or cancels. NOT a pop.

return_step01_impossible.description: Any RETURN attempt at Step 01 is impossible. UI doesn't offer the button. Handler has nothing to pop.

fiori_apps.description: SAPUI5 self-service apps for HR workflows (long-running)

asr_framework.description: SAP standard ASR HR forms framework, redefined with UNESCO behavior

webdynpro_legacy.description: Legacy WebDynpro ABAP components still maintained in parallel with Fiori

core_hr_project_2025_2026.description: SuccessFactors integration project 2025-2026. Concentrated development burst starting 2025-10 (80 TRs 2025 + 53 TRs 2026 YTD = 133 TRs on HR Fiori apps by 4-6 editors). Target: end state 2026 = hybrid SFSF + retained UNESCO custom apps. This subtopic IS the SFSF migration project.

returning_editors.N_MENARD: Historical primary maintainer (158 TRs 2022-2026), continues through project

core_hr_project_2025_2026.cross_domain_note: S_IGUENINNI's 95 total transports 2025-2026 include both HR-Fiori (~40) and non-HR content (~55 larger bundles with ABAP methods, data elements, web apps). The 8 that include PSCC/PCYC objects were previously classified as PY-Finance collateral - they belong here in HR-Workflows / Core HR Project, not in PY-Finance.
